original_hypothesis.title: Marketplace Freeze Prevents Emergency Unlisting Including by Admin
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The unlistVesting function has the isFreeze modifier, which prevents ALL callers (including s2Admin) from unlisting when the marketplace is frozen. This contradicts the emergency design intent where the admin should be able to unlist vestings during a freeze to protect user funds. Sellers' listed vesting tokens are locked indefinitely during freeze.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The isFreeze modifier fires before the function body executes. The admin authorization check is inside the function body. During freeze, the modifier reverts before reaching the admin check, making admin unlisting impossible without first unfreezing (which creates an insecure window).

proof_of_signal.pattern: function unlistVesting(...) external isFreeze { — modifier blocks all callers before admin check at line 217

preconditions: Admin has frozen the marketplace via setMarketplaceStatus(true)
Active listings exist with non-zero balances

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: isFreeze modifier on unlistVesting prevents admin emergency recovery
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Documentation states admin should be able to delist vestings during emergency, but code prevents it
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Admin bypass of freeze check for unlistVesting is missing

analysis_questions: Can admin workaround by unfreezing, unlisting, refreezing atomically?
During the unfreeze window, can other users exploit the temporary unfreeze?
Are there scenarios where the admin cannot unfreeze (key loss, governance delay)?
validation_requirements: Attempt admin unlistVesting during freeze and confirm revert
Verify that all listed tokens are inaccessible during freeze
Test unfreeze-unlist-refreeze sequence for race conditions

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Admin freezes marketplace due to emergency. All sellers with active listings cannot recover their vesting allocations. Admin must unfreeze to perform emergency unlisting, but this window allows other marketplace operations (purchases, new listings) that the freeze was meant to prevent.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: All listed vesting tokens locked during freeze with no emergency recovery path
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All sellers with active listings at time of freeze
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires unfreezing marketplace, creating security window

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for admin unlisting during marketplace freeze

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Obvious when admin attempts to unlist during freeze

validation_result.mechanism: freeze modifier blocks admin emergency unlist
validation_result.impact_summary: When marketplace is frozen, the admin cannot unlist any vestings because the isFreeze modifier reverts before the admin authorization check in the function body, locking all listed vesting tokens indefinitely during freeze.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The unlistVesting function applies the isFreeze modifier, which checks `!IMarketplaceSetting(marketplaceSetting).isMarketplaceFreeze()` and reverts if the marketplace is frozen. The admin authorization check (`msg.sender == IMarketplaceSetting(marketplaceSetting).s2Admin()`) is inside the function body, which executes AFTER the modifier. Therefore, when the marketplace is frozen, no caller—including the admin—can reach the function body. The admin's only workaround is to unfreeze the marketplace, perform the unlist, and re-freeze. However, this unfreeze window allows other marketplace operations (spotPurchase, listVesting) that the freeze was intended to block, creating a race condition where malicious actors could front-run the admin's unlist transaction with purchases or listings during the temporary unfreeze.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The isFreeze modifier is defined in SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol and checks the freeze flag before allowing execution. The unlistVesting function applies this modifier. The admin check is inside the function body, unreachable during freeze. Both listVesting and spotPurchase also use isFreeze, so unfreezing to unlist opens the door to all marketplace operations.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This modifier blocks ALL callers, including the admin, when the marketplace is frozen. It does not differentiate between regular users and the admin.

The isFreeze modifier fires before the function body. The admin authorization check at the require statement is inside the body. During freeze, the modifier reverts before the admin check is ever evaluated, making admin emergency unlisting impossible.
The admin can toggle the freeze on/off, but unfreezing to perform an emergency unlist also unblocks listVesting and spotPurchase, creating a security window that the freeze was meant to prevent.

calling_sequence: 4. require(!true) fails → transaction reverts with 'SS_Marketplace: Marketplace is currently frozen'
5. Function body never reached; admin check at line 217-220 is never evaluated
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The execution terminates at the isFreeze modifier. The modifier at SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol:155-161 calls isMarketplaceFreeze() on the settings contract. When frozen (isMarketplaceFreeze == true in SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting.sol), the require statement reverts. The admin authorization check at SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol:217-220 (listing.seller == msg.sender || msg.sender == s2Admin) is inside the function body (after the modifier's `_;`), so it is never reached. This means the code at lines 221-248 that handles unlisting logic including VestingManager.unlistVesting() is completely inaccessible during freeze for any caller.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this issue. The isFreeze modifier is hardcoded on unlistVesting and the freeze state is controlled by setMarketplaceStatus. Deployment scripts show standard marketplace setup without any special freeze-bypass mechanism.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) Admin freezes marketplace via setMarketplaceStatus(true) due to a security incident. (2) Multiple sellers have active listings with vesting tokens locked in VestingManager. (3) Admin attempts to call unlistVesting to return tokens to sellers as an emergency measure. (4) Transaction reverts at the isFreeze modifier. (5) Admin is forced to call setMarketplaceStatus(false) to unfreeze. (6) During the unfreeze window, a mempool watcher front-runs with spotPurchase calls, executing trades the freeze was supposed to prevent. (7) Admin's unlistVesting executes, but damage is done from the front-run trades. (8) Admin re-freezes, but the security window has been exploited. This is a realistic scenario especially in environments with MEV or mempool visibility.
validation_result.impact_assessment: All sellers with active listings at the time of a marketplace freeze have their vesting tokens locked indefinitely. The admin cannot perform emergency recovery without first unfreezing, which exposes the marketplace to the exact operations the freeze was meant to prevent. In the worst case, if the admin freezes due to a discovered exploit, unfreezing to recover tokens reopens the exploit window. The financial impact equals the total value of all listed vesting tokens at the time of freeze.
recommended_mitigations: Add an admin bypass to the isFreeze modifier or create a separate emergency unlist function that bypasses the freeze check but is restricted to admin only
Alternative: Create a separate emergencyUnlist function without the isFreeze modifier, gated by require(msg.sender == s2Admin)
Consider using a more granular freeze mechanism that can selectively block listing/purchasing while allowing unlisting
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full marketplace stack (MarketplaceSetting, VestingManager, Marketplace, StepVesting). 2. Create a vesting plan and allocate tokens to a seller. 3. Seller calls listVesting to create an active listing. 4. Admin calls setMarketplaceStatus(true) to freeze. 5. Admin calls unlistVesting - expect revert with 'Marketplace is currently frozen'. 6. Seller calls unlistVesting - expect same revert. 7. Verify tokens remain locked in VestingManager. 8. Admin unfreezes, verifies other users can now call spotPurchase during the window.
uncertainties: Whether the design intent was for admin to have emergency unlist capability during freeze (code comments reference '3.4. The s2Admin is unable to unlist vesting' suggesting this was a known issue that was partially addressed but not fully resolved)
additional_testing_needed: Test the unfreeze-unlist-refreeze sequence for front-running race conditions
Verify that no alternative admin path exists to recover listed tokens during freeze
Test whether the VestingManager has any direct emergency recovery mechanism bypassing the Marketplace
